root_cause_identified.expected_solution: Agent must identify that the Galaxy collection installation path in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py and lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py performs tar extraction with a custom tar member cache that is fragile under malformed or specially-crafted collection tarballs (e.g. duplicate or out-of-order members, paths that escape the destination directory, or non-regular member types). The fragility lives in how members are filtered and re-emitted to the extractor rather than relying on Python's tarfile defaults.
evaluation_criteria: Names lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py as the install entry point
Names lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py as the artifact / tar handler
Identifies the custom tar member cache (or member-filter logic) as the fragile region
Explains the failure mode in concrete terms (e.g. duplicate or path-escaping tar members surviving the filter)

regression_test_written.expected_solution: Agent writes a self-contained pytest module named regression_test.py under /workspace that constructs a deliberately-malformed Galaxy collection tarball (duplicate members, path-escaping members, or out-of-order entries) and invokes the affected installation/extraction code from lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py (or lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py). The test asserts the precise behavior the fix is meant to enforce — extraction must reject or sanitize the malformed members rather than silently accept them.
evaluation_criteria: Test file regression_test.py exists in the /workspace artifact directory
Test imports and exercises the real Galaxy collection install / artifact-manager code (no pure mocks)
Test constructs a malformed collection tarball as fixture input (duplicate members, path traversal, etc.)
Test contains at least one assertion that fails on the buggy code path
Test is runnable via pytest under /workspace without external fixtures

test_fails_on_buggy_code.expected_solution: When pytest is executed against the unpatched ansible v2.16.0 source tree under /workspace/ansible, the regression_test.py file fails with a deterministic assertion error tied to the tar fragility — for example a duplicate member overwriting a sibling, a path-traversal member escaping the install directory, or the extractor accepting a non-regular tar member. The failure must point at the Galaxy collection install path, not at unrelated infrastructure.
evaluation_criteria: pytest exits non-zero on the unpatched code
The failing assertion lives in regression_test.py under /workspace and names the Galaxy collection install/extract behavior
The failure trace touches lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py or lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py
The failure is deterministic across reruns (no flakiness, no env-dependent assumptions)
